sections.title: Credits
sections.notes: Standard publication credits.

sections.title: Contents
sections.notes: Table of contents.

sections.title: Framing Fiction – Listen
sections.notes: Opening framing fiction establishing the narrative voice and the transmission of clan history through the Cobweb.

sections.title: The Birth of Madness
sections.notes: Introduction to the clan's origin and the nature of Malkav's curse.

sections.title: Malkav
sections.notes: The story of Malkav, his relationship to Caine and the Third Generation, and the conflicting accounts of his origin.

sections.title: The Curse
sections.notes: Various accounts of how Malkav was cursed with the Sight and how this curse spread to all his childer.

sections.title: Madness in the Old Times
sections.notes: How the mad were treated in ancient times and the clan's early struggles for survival.

sections.title: Brother Saulot and Brother Set: A Parable
sections.anchor: ## BrOthEr SauLOt and BrOthEr SEt A parable:
sections.notes: A parable about Malkav's relationship with Saulot and Set, exploring different paths to wisdom.

sections.title: The Methuselahs
sections.notes: Malkav's childer of the Fourth Generation and the sacred number that governs them.

sections.title: The Plague-Bride
sections.notes: The story of Malkav's first childe, who began the creed of infection and appears as a willing Typhoid Mary.

sections.title: Nissiku, the Clever Prince
sections.notes: The story of Nissiku, one of Malkav's first childer, who could reach through reality's skin and is known by many names.

sections.title: The Eater
sections.notes: The story of a Methuselah who learned to eat names, devouring them entirely and feeding on their power.

sections.title: The Severing of Malkav
sections.notes: The story of how Malkav was torn apart near Petra and how his consciousness fragmented into the Cobweb.

sections.title: The Growth of the Clan
sections.notes: How the clan expanded after Malkav's severing, with the Methuselahs rising as demigods in their own right.

sections.title: The Old Cities
sections.notes: The clan's presence in ancient Mesopotamia, Greece, and other early civilizations.

sections.title: Rome
sections.notes: The clan's experiences in Rome, including their relationship with Prince Camilla and the city's eventual fall.

sections.title: Carthage
sections.notes: The clan's role in the destruction of Carthage, including Tryphosa's prophecy and the Dionysian's influence.

sections.title: Death of an Empire
sections.notes: The fall of the Roman Empire and the clan's relationship with the mad emperors and the chaos of the time.

sections.title: The Long Night
sections.notes: The medieval period when vampires ruled openly, and the clan's role as advisors and observers rather than rulers.

sections.title: The Death of Brother Saulot
sections.notes: The clan's perspective on Saulot's death at the hands of the Tremere and why they didn't join the war against the Usurpers.

sections.title: The First Crusade
sections.notes: The fall of Jerusalem in 1099 and how the bloodshed and madness drew Malkavians to the Holy Land.

sections.title: Plague
sections.notes: The Black Death and how it affected both mortals and Kindred, with warnings about future plagues.

sections.title: The Anarchs
sections.notes: The clan's relationship with the Anarch Revolt and their attempts to show the rebels the emptiness of their cause.

sections.title: The Burning
sections.notes: The Inquisition and how the clan suffered during the fires, with the Cobweb saving many through warnings.

sections.title: Birth of the Camarilla
sections.notes: The formation of the Camarilla and the clan's near-exclusion, saved by their insight and the efforts of Unmada and Vasantasena.

sections.title: Unmada and Vasantasena
sections.notes: The two Malkavians from the Orient who brought the clan together and helped form the Camarilla.

sections.title: The Convention of Thorns
sections.notes: The gathering at Thorns where the anarchs were brought to heel, and Vasantasena's speech condemning the Convention.

sections.title: Clan Heraldry
sections.notes: The story of how Malkavians created the heraldic symbols for all the clans through the work of Pelinka, an autistic child.

sections.title: The Split
sections.notes: The gathering at Domazlice where six Methuselahs placed mental blocks on Camarilla Malkavians, dampening their infectious madness.

sections.title: The Great Prank
sections.notes: The feat of replacing Dementation with Dominate clan-wide, and theories about how it was accomplished.

sections.title: After the Inquisition
sections.notes: The Renaissance and how the clan fared during the age of asylums and institutionalization.

sections.title: The Degeneration of the 'Antitribu'
sections.notes: Why Sabbat Malkavians are so psychotic and how the sect's practices have amplified their madness.

sections.title: The New World
sections.notes: The clan's initial experiences in the Americas and their decision to wait for cities rather than follow early colonists.

sections.title: Mob Rule
sections.notes: The French Revolution and how the madness and bloodshed drew Malkavians to Paris.

sections.title: The 19th Century
sections.notes: The Industrial Revolution, the growth of cities, and the expansion of asylums.

sections.title: The Age of Victoria
sections.notes: The Victorian era and how the repression of emotion created cracks that Malkavians found fascinating.

sections.title: Modern Nights
sections.notes: The 20th century, including the World Wars, the Depression, and the atomic age.

sections.title: Reawakening
sections.notes: The Reawakening of 1997 when the blocks placed at Domazlice loosened and Dementation returned to Camarilla Malkavians.

sections.title: The Week of Nightmares
sections.notes: Ravnos' awakening and death, and how his fever burned across the Cobweb, affecting all Malkavians.

sections.title: Gehenna
sections.notes: Warnings about the coming end times and the Antediluvians' awakening.

sections.title: The Family Malkavian
sections.notes: Overview of the clan's structure, or lack thereof, and how they relate to one another.

sections.title: The Embrace
sections.notes: How Malkavians choose childer and what happens during the Embrace.

sections.title: Before and After
sections.notes: How the Embrace changes mortals and what they gain and lose.

sections.title: The Gnawed
sections.notes: Malkavians who have been partially consumed or damaged by the Cobweb or their own madness.

sections.title: Gatherings
sections.notes: How Malkavians gather, the Call, and what happens at clan meetings.

sections.title: The Camarilla
sections.notes: The clan's relationship with the Camarilla, their role as advisors and seers, and their barely tolerated status.

sections.title: The Sabbat
sections.notes: The Sabbat 'antitribu' and how their madness has been amplified by sect practices.

sections.title: Autarkis
sections.notes: Malkavians who stand outside all sects and follow their own visions.

sections.title: The Cobweb
sections.notes: The Madness Network that connects all Malkavians, its nature, and how it functions.

sections.title: Malkavian Practices
sections.notes: Common practices and behaviors among the clan.

sections.title: Pranking
sections.notes: The art of pranking as both communication and revelation.

sections.title: Prophecy
sections.notes: The clan's role as prophets and seers, and how they deliver their visions.

sections.title: Pranks and Prestige
sections.notes: How pranks can build or destroy reputation within the clan.

sections.title: The 'Antitribu' Perspective
sections.notes: How Sabbat Malkavians view themselves and their Camarilla cousins.

sections.title: Reality Busting
sections.notes: How Malkavians break through illusions and reveal hidden truths.

sections.title: Elders and Children
sections.notes: The relationship between elder and neonate Malkavians.

sections.title: Pockets of Madness
sections.notes: Places where madness pools and Malkavians gather, such as asylums and institutions.

sections.title: Clan Traditions
sections.notes: Formal and informal traditions within the clan.

sections.title: The Real Clan Tradition
sections.notes: The true tradition that unites all Malkavians despite their apparent chaos.

sections.title: Salvation
sections.notes: The clan's views on redemption, Golconda, and spiritual matters.

sections.title: The World Outside
sections.notes: How Malkavians interact with mortals and the mortal world.

sections.title: Assamites
sections.notes: The clan's relationship with the Assamites.

sections.title: Brujah
sections.notes: The clan's relationship with the Brujah.

sections.notes: The clan's relationship with the Followers of Set.

sections.title: The Human Element
sections.notes: The clan's relationship with mortals and humanity.

sections.title: Where the Malkavians Are
sections.notes: Geographic distribution and common havens of the clan.

sections.title: The Anatomy of Madness
sections.notes: Introduction to derangements and how they work for Malkavians.

sections.title: The Real Thing
sections.notes: Advice on playing derangements seriously and realistically.

sections.title: Roleplaying a Lunatic
sections.notes: Guidelines for roleplaying Malkavian characters and their derangements.

sections.title: Derangements
sections.notes: General information about derangements and how they affect Malkavians.

sections.title: New Derangements
sections.notes: Clan-specific derangements unique to Malkavians.

sections.title: Desensitization
sections.notes: A derangement that makes the vampire an emotional amputee.

sections.title: Dissociative Blood-Spending
sections.notes: A derangement that causes unconscious spending of vitae.

sections.title: Masochism
sections.notes: A derangement that associates pain with pleasure.

sections.title: Memory Lapses
sections.notes: A derangement that causes random memory loss.

sections.title: Power-Object Fixation
sections.notes: A derangement that causes obsession with a specific object.

sections.title: Regression
sections.notes: A derangement that causes reversion to childlike state under stress.

sections.title: Synesthesia
sections.notes: A derangement that scrambles sensory input.

sections.notes: Detailed explanation of the Cobweb and how it functions.

sections.title: The Particulars of Insight
sections.notes: Alternative to the Network using the Insight Background from Time of Thin Blood.

sections.notes: Malkavian Time—the Background/Talent that represents connection to the Cobweb.
